subject: Re: BoxNet picking issues with UltraAuFoil grids
body:
Hi Dimitry,

Thanks for the response. I am attempting to use 1.1.0 beta, but I am having 
an issue where every movie is deselected and, therefore, won't process 
anything. With the same settings (but different boxnet model), 1.0.9 can 
process the movies. Also, after a few attempts in 1.1.0, the program cannot 
detect the movie path anymore (this was fixed with a restart).  Do you have 
any idea why all my movies are deselected in 1.1.0, but no 1.0.9?

Thanks,
Matt

On Saturday, September 24, 2022 at 12:08:17 PM UTC-4 teg...@gmail.com wrote:

> Hi Both,
>
> Have you tried 1.1.0 already? It should do better in this case. The 
> contrast is off in the viewer because 1.0.9 estimates mean and standard 
> deviation of the whole micrograph, so the gold region throws it off. 1.1.0 
> uses a smaller central region, so should be less affected. CTF estimation 
> isn't involved in any of this.
>
> Cheers,
> Dimitry
>
> On Saturday, September 24, 2022 at 5:38:57 AM UTC-7 b.m.ha...@gmail.com 
> wrote:
>
>> I think that is best answered by Dimitry. We haven't had much luck 
>> playing with the CTF-estimation parameters for Ultraufoils and we don't get 
>> the same problems with grids where the foil is made from lighter metals and 
>> alloys.
>>
>> On Friday, September 23, 2022 at 4:43:45 PM UTC+3 mjja...@gmail.com 
>> wrote:
>>
>>> Thanks for the response Martin.
>>>
>>> Do you thing this is due to the BoxNet picker or the CTF estimation? The 
>>> contrast of micrographs with significant gold looks off, which suggests to 
>>> me that there is a CTF estimation issue.
>>>
>>> Thanks,
>>> Matt
>>>
>>> On Fri, Sep 23, 2022, 12:04 AM Martin Hallberg <b.m.ha...@gmail.com> 
>>> wrote:
>>>
>>>> Hi Matt,
>>>> This is common with Warp BoxNet particle picking. We try to avoid to 
>>>> have the gold in the image but if we anyway end up with it on a part of the 
>>>> data we pick using other tools. If needed for further processing in the 
>>>> Warp/M suite, one can always import particles (or just positions) to Warp 
>>>> usually then after some 2D or 3D "cleaning". I would love to hear if 
>>>> someone has a solution!
>>>>
>>>> Best,
>>>>
>>>> Martin
>>>>
>>>>
>>>>
>>>> On Thursday, September 22, 2022 at 8:00:39 PM UTC+3 mjja...@gmail.com 
>>>> wrote:
>>>>
>>>>> Hello,
>>>>>
>>>>> I'm having BoxNet picking issues when there is a considerable amount 
>>>>> of the UltraAuFoil grid scaffold in the images:
>>>>>
>>>>> BoxNet seems to be missing a lot of particles. This doesn’t happen 
>>>>> when less gold is in the image. Any way to fix this?
>>>>>
>>>>> Here are my CTF settings:
>>>>>
>>>>>
>>>>> Thank you,
>>>>>
>>>>> Matt
>>>>>
>>>>> --

>>>> .
>>>>
>>>
